title: sonchus bulbosus subsp. microcephalus
intro: Sonchus bulbosus subsp. microcephalus, also known as the lesser sow thistle, is a subspecies of thistle in the dandelion family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sonchus bulbosus subsp. microcephalus include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be consumed raw in salads or as a vegetable.
